incidence.value: N/A (chronic condition; new cases tracked via screening)

burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease; SBC

riskFactors: unhealthy diet high in saturated fats
vulnerablePopulations: indigenous and rural populations in Amazon region
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Total cholesterol >200 mg/dL, LDL-C >130 mg/dL, HDL-C <40 mg/dL (men) or <50 mg/dL (women), triglycerides >150 mg/dL; per Brazilian Society of Cardiology (SBC) guidelines

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Aligned with national SBC guidelines; lipid profile testing via SUS labs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle modification first; statins (e.g., simvastatin, atorvastatin) available free via SUS for high-risk patients; follow-up with lipid panels every 6-12 months
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Disparities in access between South/Southeast (higher screening) and North/Northeast (lower due to infrastructure); high prevalence in urban areas like São Paulo (30%) vs. rural (20%)
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Integration with Family Health Strategy (Estratégia Saúde da Família) for screening; national statin distribution program reducing costs; SBC awareness campaigns
